title: Fiery Spicy Venezuelan Chicken Empanadas
description: Sour and snappy empanadas filled with spicy chicken and served with a side of tangy sauce, perfect for a brunch mood, these empanadas are gluten-free friendly and packed with flavor. The render fat adds a rich and savory element to the dish.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 375 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a large skillet, heat the rendered fat over medium heat.
Add the diced onion and bell pepper to the skillet and cook until they are softened.
Add the chicken breast to the skillet and cook until it is browned and cooked through.
In a large bowl, combine the cooked chicken, hot sauce, and lime juice.
Roll out the empanada dough and cut out circles of dough.
Place a tablespoon of the chicken mixture onto the center of each dough circle.
Fold the dough in half and press the edges together to seal the empanada.
Brush the tops of the empanadas with the rendered fat and bake for 20 minutes.
Serve the empanadas hot with a side of tangy sauce made from the remaining lime juice and hot sauce.
